_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska State Representatives (HD9-13, Anchorage). Sitting members w/ voting records. Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no public position. Notable: Kopp (R) is Majority Leader of the Dem-led coalition (cross-party); Josephson is RETIRING (candidacy fixed).

ky-holland.notes_set: AK House District 9 (S. Anchorage Hillside/Girdwood). Independent in the Democrat-led bipartisan House majority coalition (14 D, 5 I, 2 R); co-chairs House Special Committee on Energy. Self-describes 'progressive on economic matters.' Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates (2024). Declined the Alaska Family Action Values Voter survey. First-term member (elected 2024); thin recorded roll-call history. Running 2026 (John Boyle challenging). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (autopilot, AK-leg batch). Most conservative-rubric cells genuinely NO EVIDENCE FOUND -> null.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates-Alaska (2024); named among pro-abortion candidates the group helped elect.

chuck-kopp.notes_set: AK House District 10 (SW Anchorage/Oceanview). Republican, but House MAJORITY LEADER since Jan 2025 as 1 of only 2 Republicans in the 21-member Democrat-led coalition (Speaker Bryce Edgmon, I). Former Kenai chief of police (~20 yrs law enforcement). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION case: the prior 97/100 archetype scorecard was uncited and is contradicted by the primary record. Documented: in the coalition bloc that voted DOWN Medicaid-abortion-funding restriction (first time in 25+ yrs; the 20 YES were the R minority); BUT voted FOR the amendment barring Medicaid funding of gender-dysphoria/transgender care (passed 21-19). Drove higher public-school (BSA) spending + the education-veto override. A hostile conservative outlet says he defended SB 91 (soft-on-crime) -> left null pending the actual vote. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (autopilot, AK-leg batch).

1.note: As Majority Leader of the coalition bloc that voted DOWN budget language restricting Medicaid abortion funding (failed 20-21; all 20 YES were the Republican minority). Not an abortion-restriction/abolition supporter.

2.note: Voted FOR the budget amendment barring state Medicaid funding for gender-dysphoria/transgender care (passed 21-19).

0.note: Career law enforcement: former Kenai chief of police (~20 yrs); no defund/dismantle-police stance documented.

julie-coulombe.notes_set: AK House District 11 (S. Anchorage). Republican in the GOP minority (declined the bipartisan majority coalition: 'It's a Democrat caucus with a couple of ours... I have a real struggle with their priorities and values'). Running 2026 ('Re-Elect Julie Coulombe'). Self-describes pro-life; voted FOR the Eastman amendment to bar Medicaid funding of abortion (2023). Supports school choice (charter + homeschool). Fiscal restraint ('Alaska must learn to live within its means'). Supported repealing ranked-choice voting (Measure 2, 2024) and opposed a $15 min wage. Documented fiscal/education CROSSOVER: voted YES to override Dunleavy's HB57 education veto (Aug 2025). Note: voted for HB17 birth-control-access (PP-backed) and urged 'nuance' on drug sentencing - flagged but not scored. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (autopilot, AK-leg batch).

0.note: Self-describes pro-life; voted FOR the Eastman amendment to bar Medicaid funding of abortion (HB39, 2023).
Campaign Issues page: supports funding for 'neighborhood schools, charter schools, and homeschool programs because families deserve strong educational options.'

2.note: Fiscal restraint: 'Alaska must learn to live within its means'; supports reduced spending to lower taxpayer burden; opposed a $15 minimum-wage mandate.

calvin-schrage.notes_set: AK House District 12 (Anchorage/Abbott Loop). Registered Independent; former House Minority Leader (2023-25) who joined the Democrat-led majority coalition in 2025 as a House Finance Committee co-chair. Self-identifies Christian but emphasizes 'the separation between church and state is the bedrock of the nation.' Documented progressive record: voted to TABLE/kill the Eastman amendment barring Medicaid abortion funding (2023); 'proudly endorsed' by Planned Parenthood; opposed Dunleavy's 'don't say gay'-style education bill ('We don't need Florida's politics in Alaska'); defends ranked-choice voting; says he hunts/owns guns but supports 'strengthening background checks.' DISPROVED two false leads: he is NOT an RCV-repeal sponsor (co-sponsors a campaign-contribution-limits initiative instead) and is NOT a 2026 congressional candidate. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (autopilot, AK-leg batch).

1.note: Voted to table/kill Rep. Eastman's amendment that would have barred state Medicaid funds from paying for abortions (2023; failed 23-17, Schrage with the 23).

4.note: 'Proudly endorsed' by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates; named among the pro-abortion candidates the group helped elect.
As Minority Leader opposed Gov. Dunleavy's 'don't say gay'-style education bill: 'We don't need Florida's politics in Alaska.'

2.note: Opposed the Dunleavy 'don't say gay'-style curriculum-restriction bill, against limiting SOGI content in schools.

1.note: Candidate Q&A: owns firearms/hunts but supports 'strengthening background checks' and gun-safety measures - a gun-control lean.

andy-josephson.notes_set: AK House District 13 (Anchorage/Campbell). Democrat; House Finance Committee co-chair in the 2025 coalition; in office since 2013. RETIRING at the end of his term (serves through Jan 2027) - NOT seeking reelection in 2026; open seat contested by two Democrats (Felix Rivera, Lisa Keller). Conservative Freedom Index cumulative score ~4%. Documented progressive record: pro-choice (questioned the anti-abortion-pill lawsuit; voted NO on telehealth abortion-drug restriction), sponsor of LGBTQ anti-discrimination bills + a same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment, sponsor of HB162 red-flag firearm law, sanctuary 'Trust Act' advocate. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: the only House Democrat to vote NO on SB91 (2016 lenient justice-reinvestment law) - genuinely tough-on-crime, self-brands 'Public Safety Champion' (former assistant DA). (Freedom Index records a YES on a women's-sports/biological-sex bill - unverified and conflicts with his broader record, so left null.) Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (autopilot, AK-leg batch).

1.note: Pro-choice: publicly questioned Alaska joining the anti-abortion-pill lawsuit ('punitive... the whole procedure is legal here'); Freedom Index records NO on restricting telehealth abortion drugs.

4.note: Named among the pro-abortion electeds celebrated by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates-Alaska.

0.note: Sponsored a constitutional amendment to recognize same-sex marriage - rejects man-woman-only definition.

1.note: Sponsored a same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment - supports, not opposes, same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Lead sponsor every year of LGBTQ anti-discrimination bills covering gender identity/expression (e.g. HB82) - embraces, not rejects, transgender-protective policy.

4.note: Lead sponsor of statewide LGBTQ anti-discrimination legislation in housing/employment/public accommodations - promotes LGBTQ identity in policy.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY: the only House Democrat to vote NO on SB91, the 2016 lenient justice-reinvestment ('catch-and-release') law - opposed the soft-on-crime measure (former assistant DA).

2.note: Advocates Connecticut-style 'Trust Act' sanctuary legislation limiting state cooperation with ICE: state 'will not participate in efforts to bring people to justice who have sought sanctuary here.'

1.note: Sponsor of HB162, an Extreme Risk / red-flag firearm-removal law (NRA/2A groups opposed); Freedom Index records NO on pro-gun-rights amendments.
